tree.setup: A maze of old alleys in a historic town. Your phone died ten minutes ago and you're thoroughly lost. The alleys all look the same -- stone walls, red lanterns, and cats who watch you with disinterest. An elderly man, Uncle Qiu, is sitting on a bamboo stool outside his door, playing chess against himself. He has lived here for sixty years and has opinions about tourists, navigation apps, and the superiority of asking real people.
tree.setup_zh: 古镇里迷宫般的老巷子。你的手机十分钟前没电了，你已经彻底迷路了。巷子看起来都一样——石墙、红灯笼和漠不关心地看着你的猫。一位老人邱叔坐在门口的竹凳上，自己跟自己下棋。他在这里住了六十年，对游客、导航App和问真人的优越性有自己的看法。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 又一个迷路的。手机没电了吧？过来坐。你要去哪里？
turns.text_pinyin: yòu yí gè mí lù de. shǒujī méi diàn le ba? guòlái zuò. nǐ yào qù nǎlǐ?
turns.text_en: Another lost one. Phone died, right? Come sit. Where are you trying to go?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Explain where you need to go.

options.text_zh: 我想回主街。从那里可以找到我住的客栈。叫'清风客栈'，您知道吗？
options.text_pinyin: wǒ xiǎng huí zhǔjiē. cóng nàlǐ kěyǐ zhǎo dào wǒ zhù de kèzhàn. jiào 'qīngfēng kèzhàn', nín zhīdào ma?
options.text_en: I want to get back to the main street. From there I can find my guesthouse. It's called 'Clear Wind Inn,' do you know it?
options.feedback: Uncle Qiu nods slowly.「清风客栈？老王家开的。从这里走不远。」Giving a specific destination and landmark name helps immensely. 客栈 is 'inn/guesthouse.' 主街 is 'main street.'

options.text_zh: 我迷路了。这里是哪里？附近有没有大一点的路？
options.text_pinyin: wǒ mí lù le. zhèlǐ shì nǎlǐ? fùjìn yǒu méiyǒu dà yìdiǎn de lù?
options.text_en: I'm lost. Where is this? Are there any bigger roads nearby?
options.feedback: 「这里叫后巷。你从哪个方向来的？」Uncle Qiu needs to calibrate. 后巷 is 'back alley.' Without a specific destination, directions are harder to give.

options.text_zh: 您好！请问有没有地方可以给手机充电？
options.text_pinyin: nín hǎo! qǐngwèn yǒu méiyǒu dìfang kěyǐ gěi shǒujī chōng diàn?
options.text_en: Hello! Is there somewhere I can charge my phone?
options.feedback: Uncle Qiu waves toward his door.「进来充吧。」He has a single outlet by the entrance. 充电 is 'charge.' Solving the phone problem first is practical but Uncle Qiu can navigate you faster than any app.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 清风客栈我知道。但是你不能按导航走，导航不认识这些小巷子。听我说：从这里往前走，看到一口井右转。然后一直走到一棵大榕树，左转就是主街了。
turns.text_pinyin: qīngfēng kèzhàn wǒ zhīdào. dànshì nǐ bù néng àn dǎoháng zǒu, dǎoháng bú rènshi zhèxiē xiǎo xiàngzi. tīng wǒ shuō: cóng zhèlǐ wǎng qián zǒu, kàn dào yì kǒu jǐng yòu zhuǎn. ránhòu yìzhí zǒu dào yì kē dà róngshu, zuǒ zhuǎn jiùshì zhǔjiē le.
turns.text_en: I know Clear Wind Inn. But you can't follow GPS, GPS doesn't know these little alleys. Listen: from here go forward, when you see a well turn right. Then walk straight until you reach a big banyan tree, turn left and you're on the main street.

options.text_zh: 好，我重复一下：往前走，看到井右转，到大树左转。对吗？大概走几分钟？
options.text_pinyin: hǎo, wǒ chóngfù yíxià: wǎng qián zǒu, kàn dào jǐng yòu zhuǎn, dào dà shù zuǒ zhuǎn. duì ma? dàgài zǒu jǐ fēnzhōng?
options.text_en: OK, let me repeat: go forward, see the well turn right, big tree turn left. Correct? About how many minutes' walk?
options.feedback: Uncle Qiu nods approvingly.「对。五六分钟。」He's pleased you repeated back. 重复 is 'repeat.' Using landmarks instead of street names is how locals navigate old towns.

options.text_zh: 井？什么样的井？这里还有井吗？
options.text_pinyin: jǐng? shénme yàng de jǐng? zhèlǐ hái yǒu jǐng ma?
options.text_en: A well? What kind of well? There are still wells here?
options.feedback: 「石头的，上面有盖子。以前全镇喝水都靠它。」Uncle Qiu looks faintly nostalgic. 井 is 'well.' 盖子 is 'cover.' The old infrastructure becomes your navigation.

options.text_zh: 能不能帮我画一个简单的地图？我怕走到一半忘了。
options.text_pinyin: néng bu néng bāng wǒ huà yí gè jiǎndān de dìtú? wǒ pà zǒu dào yíbàn wàng le.
options.text_en: Could you draw me a simple map? I'm afraid I'll forget halfway.
options.feedback: Uncle Qiu picks up a twig and draws in the dust.「看——这是我们，这是井，这是树。」简单 is 'simple.' 一半 is 'halfway.' A dust map is more reliable than you'd think.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 年轻人，下次来之前先把地图截个图。手机没电了就什么都完了。以前的人哪有导航？全靠嘴巴问路。
turns.text_pinyin: niánqīngrén, xià cì lái zhīqián xiān bǎ dìtú jié gè tú. shǒujī méi diàn le jiù shénme dōu wán le. yǐqián de rén nǎ yǒu dǎoháng? quán kào zuǐba wèn lù.
turns.text_en: Young person, next time screenshot the map before you come. Once the phone dies, you're done for. People in the old days had no GPS -- they relied entirely on asking for directions.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his advice before leaving.

options.text_zh: 您说得对。其实问路比看手机有意思多了。谢谢您，邱叔！下棋赢了吗？
options.text_pinyin: nín shuō de duì. qíshí wèn lù bǐ kàn shǒujī yǒu yìsi duō le. xièxie nín, qiū shū! xià qí yíng le ma?
options.text_en: You're right. Actually, asking for directions is much more interesting than looking at a phone. Thank you, Uncle Qiu! Did you win the chess game?
options.feedback: Uncle Qiu chuckles.「自己跟自己下，当然赢了。」有意思多了 is 'much more interesting.' Asking about his chess game shows you noticed what he was doing. He waves you off warmly.

options.text_zh: 谢谢您！这个古镇真的很美。您住在这里这么久，一定有很多故事吧。
options.text_pinyin: xièxie nín! zhè ge gǔzhèn zhēn de hěn měi. nín zhù zài zhèlǐ zhème jiǔ, yídìng yǒu hěn duō gùshi ba.
options.text_en: Thank you! This old town is really beautiful. You've lived here so long, you must have many stories.
options.feedback: Uncle Qiu's eyes soften.「故事？太多了。下次你再来，我慢慢跟你说。」古镇 is 'ancient town.' Inviting someone's stories is the highest form of respect for elders.

options.text_zh: 记住了，下次截图。谢谢您，我赶紧走了！
options.text_pinyin: jìzhù le, xià cì jiétú. xièxie nín, wǒ gǎnjǐn zǒu le!
options.text_en: Got it, screenshot next time. Thanks, I'd better hurry!
options.feedback: 「慢慢走！别急！」Uncle Qiu calls after you. 赶紧 is 'hurry up.' He watches you go, then returns to his chess game. One more tourist saved.

tree.cultural_note: Ancient towns (古镇, gǔzhèn) in China often have alleys too narrow for cars and too winding for GPS. Local landmarks -- wells (井), trees (树), temples (庙) -- are more reliable than street names. Asking for directions (问路, wèn lù) from elderly residents is both practical and a cultural experience. Always screenshot your map (截图) before your phone dies.
